title: Branch Prediction
summary: Explains static and dynamic branch prediction schemes, the Intel Pentium BTB with 2-bit saturation counters, and trace caches.
content_points: Goal: foresee jump outcome to avoid pipeline flush; wrong prediction → rollback effects.
Static: always taken (~60%), never taken (~40%), or backward-taken/forward-not-taken.
Dynamic: BTB table records recent jump history (taken/not-taken).
Intel Pentium BTB: 4-way set-associative, 256 entries, 2-bit saturation counter (4 states: strong-hit → weak-hit → weak-miss → strong-miss).
2-bit counter: gives a second chance before changing prediction direction.
Trace cache: stores instructions in execution order, not memory order.
learning_objectives: Compare static and dynamic branch prediction
Describe the 2-bit saturation counter and its state transitions
Explain the Intel Pentium BTB implementation
